translated: If you see that your friend’s farm is on fire, you should fetch water because your farm might catch fire too.

ref: Jesus left Galilee and went to the Jordan River to be baptized by John.
text: Kɛkɛ nɛ Yesu je Galilea kɛ ba Yohane ngɔ ngɛ Yordan nya konɛ Yohane nɛ baptisi lɛ.
translated: Then Jesus came from Galilee to John at the Jordan to be baptized by John.

ref: He told them, “The Scriptures say, ‘My house should be called a place of worship.’ But you have turned it into a place where robbers hide.”
text: Nɛ e de mɛ ke: “A ngma ke: ‘A ma tsɛ ye we ɔ sɔlemi we’ se nyɛɛ lɛɛ nyɛɛ kɛ pee ojo fiali a kplo.”
translated: And he said to me: “A man says: ‘I will not go with you to the stream’ and yet he is the one who goes first.”

ref: Then Jesus went to their synagogues everywhere in Galilee, where he preached and forced out demons. Jesus Heals a Man (Matthew
text: Lɔ ɔ he ɔ, a ya kpa Galilea tsuo, nɛ e fiɛ ngɛ a kpe he ɔmɛ nɛ e hia mumi yayamihi hulɔ. Yesu tsa tsitsɛ ko (Mateo
translated: When he saw the crowds, he went up on the mountain, and after he sat down, his disciples came to him. Jesus opened his mouth and taught them (Matthew

ref: Jesus said to the crowds and to his disciples:
text: Kɛkɛ nɛ Yesu tu munyu tsɔɔ asafo ɔmɛ kɛ e kaseli ɔmɛ nɛ e de mɛ ke:
translated: When Jesus came down from the mountain, large crowds followed him.

ref: The disciples left and did what Jesus had told them to do.
text: Lɔ ɔ he ɔ kaseli ɔmɛ ho nɛ a ya pee bɔ nɛ Yesu fa mɛ ɔ.
translated: The one who believes in me will live even if he dies.

ref: There they gave Jesus some wine mixed with a drug to ease the pain. But when Jesus tasted what it was, he refused to drink it.
text: a ngɔ wai nɛ a kɛ duɔ futu mi ha lɛ ke e nu; se benɛ e tso hyɛ ɔ, e sume kaa e nuu.
translated: If you had come you would have seen the yam I planted; but since you refused, you will never see it.

ref: Then Jesus said, “Don't be afraid! Tell my followers to go to Galilee. They will see me there.” Report of the Guard
text: Kɛkɛ nɛ Yesu de mɛ ke: “Nyɛ ko ye gbeye; nyɛɛ ya nɛ nyɛ ya de ye nyɛmimɛ ɔmɛ konɛ a ya Galilea; se lejɛ ɔ a maa na mi ngɛ.” Nikɔtɔma amɛ ha buli ɔmɛ nyɔ mi mi
translated: "Then Jesus said to me: 'I am not going to you; but go to my brothers and tell them, "I am ascending to my Father and your Father, to my God and your God."' Mary Magdalene went and announced to the disciples"

ref: Every tree producing bad fruit will be chopped down and burned.
text: Tso tsuaa tso nɛ wui yiblii kpakpahi ɔ, a maa hɛ nɛ a maa ngɔ ngɔ wo la mi?
translated: If a child dies and the vultures gather, does that mean the child belongs to them?

ref: Jesus got into a boat and crossed back over to the town where he lived.
text: Kɛkɛ nɛ Yesu sɛ lɛ mi, nɛ e po wo ɔ, nɛ e ba lɛ nitsɛ e ma a mi.
translated: Truly I say to you, today you will be with me in paradise.

ref: Do something to show you have really given up your sins.
text: Ke nyɛ tsake nyɛ tsui ɔ lɛɛ nyɛɛ wo yiblii nɛ sa tsui tsakemi mɔ!
translated: If you are not a thief, then you are a witch, because no one steals from a witch!

ref: The girl hurried back and told Herod, “Here and now on a serving plate I want the head of John the Baptist!”
text: Nɛ amlɔ ɔ e ba matsɛ ɔ ngɔ oya nɛ e ba de lɛ ke: “I ngɛ hlae nɛ o ngɔ Yohane Baptisilɔ ɔ yi ngɔ ha mi amlɔ nɛ ɔ nɔuu ngɛ ka mi.”
translated: When they came to him, they said to him: “We have found that you are the one John the Baptist said would come after him.”

ref: The seeds that fell along the road are the people who hear the message. But Satan soon comes and snatches it away from them.
text: Be komɛ ɔ, munyu ɔ ya nɔɔ blɔ tue. Ni nɛ ɔmɛ nuɔ, se amlɔ nɔuu nɛ a nu ɔ, Satan baa ba saa munyu nɛ a du ngɛ a mi ɔ.
translated: When he came, he found the people living in darkness. He told them to repent, because if they did not, Satan would come and take their souls away.

ref: But while everyone was sleeping, an enemy came and scattered weed seeds in the field and then left.
text: Se benɛ ni ɔmɛ hwɔ si ɔ, e he nyɛlɔ ko ba ba du hu kɛ futu ngma a, nɛ e je.
translated: If someone sees something and it is not good for them to have, they should leave it alone.

ref: “The Emperor's,” they answered. Then Jesus told them, “Give the Emperor what belongs to him and give God what belongs to God.”
text: A de lɛ ke: “Kaisare nɔ ʼi.” Nɛ e de mɛ ke: “Lɛɛ, ynyɛɛ ngɔ Kaisare nihi ngɔ ha Kaisare, nɛ Mawu ni ɔmɛ nyɛ ngɔ ha Mawu!”
translated: He said to him: “Caesar’s is yours.” But he said to him: “No, give to Caesar what is Caesar’s, and to God what is God’s!”
